properties.remark: BLEW FOUR OF THE HUGE CARGO CONTAINERS OFF A TRAIN. THE CONTAINERS WERE EMPTY.

CURRY COUNTY EM REPORTING THUNDERSTORM WIND DAMAGE ACROSS THE TOWN OF GRADY WITH POWER POLES SNAPPED, TREES DOWN, AND ROOF DAMAGE TO BARNS. DAMAGE PATH SEEMS TO BE FROM NW TO SE SPREADING OUT ACROSS THE AREA.

CORRECTS PREVIOUS TSTM WND DMG REPORT FROM 2 NNW GRADY. CURRY COUNTY EM AND DAMAGE SURVEY TEAM FOUND 17 DOWNED ELECTRICAL TRANSMISSION LINE POLES DUE TO A MICROBURST. A COUPLE POLES WERE SNAPPED AT THE BASE AND SOME SNAPPED HALF WAY UP THE POLE. DAMAGE WAS CONSISTENT WITH 90 MPH WINDS FROM THE MICROBURST.

NWS STORM DAMAGE SURVEY DETERMINED THAT 70 MPH WIND CAUSED A CARPORT TO BE DESTROYED AND BLOWN 200 YARDS WITH A DOWNED TV ANTENNA TOWER AND STORAGE SHED TIPPED OVER ON ITS SIDE THAT WAS ATTACHED TO THE CARPORT. SEVERAL LARGE TREE LIMBS WERE DOWN ALONG WITH MOVED HAY BALES ON THE PROPERTY. DAMAGE WAS BLOWN TO THE NE ON THE NORTHERN EDGE OF A MICROBURST.

NWS STORM DAMAGE SURVEY DETERMINED THAT 70 MPH WINDS FROM A MICROBURST CAUSED MINOR ROOF DAMAGE TO METAL ROOFS PEALED BACK, LARGE LIMB/BRANCHES DOWNED, OLD TREES UPROOTED, AND A PORCH COVER ATTACHED TO A HOUSE NO LONGER ATTACHED TO IT.
SCHOOLNET OBSERVATION FROM GRADY SCHOOLS REPORTED A PEAK WIND OF 84 MPH.

AN ADULT AND 2 CHILDREN IN AN SUV WERE SWEPT UP BY FLOOD WATERS ON 18TH ST AND THEN PUSHED INTO A CULVERT HEADED SOUTH ALONG S GLOBE ST WHERE THEY EVENTUALLY CAME TO A STOP AGAINST A PIPE ACROSS THE CULVERT. PORTALES FD MADE SWIFT WATER RESCUE SAVING THE FAMILY ABOUT 45 MINUTES AFTER THE FIRST REPORT AROUND 630PM MDT.

*** 1 FATAL *** WIDESPREAD FLOODING OF ROADS AND LOW WATER CROSSINGS. ONE PERSON STUCK IN LOW WATER CROSSING AT ALAMO CANYON ROAD DROWNED WHEN TRYING TO DRIVE THEIR TRUCK THROUGH FLOODED WATERS. THE INDIVIDUAL TRIED TO ESCAPE OR FLOOD WATERS PULLED THEM FROM THEIR TRUCK. THEY WERE FOUND THE NEXT DAY.

TIJERAS ARROYO WAS AROUND 20 FEET WIDE AND 3 FEET DEEP FLOWING OVER SEDILLO RD NEAR ROUTE 66. MUD AND LARGE ROCKS WASHED OVER SEDILLO ROAD EASTWARD TO FOUR HILLS RD. WHERE ADDITIONAL FLASH FLOODING COVERED THE INTERSECTION WITH MUD... ROCKS... AND DEBRIS. ONE LARGE FENCE WAS DAMAGED.
RAINFALL RATES OF 3 TO 4 INCHES PER HOUR IN THE ABQ FOOTHILLS. THREE WATER RESCUES ONGOING IN AMAFCA FLOOD CHANNEL. ROADWAYS AND YARDS FLOODED IN THE ABQ FOOTHILLS.
EXTREME FLASH FLOODING IMPACTED CHACO CANYON WITH DAMAGE TO ROADWAYS AND GALLO CAMPGROUND. ONE SUCCESSFUL WATER RESCUE REPORTED. PARK WAS CLOSED FOR SEVERAL DAYS.
BETWEEN 300PM AMD 730PM HEAVY RAIN PRODUCED FLASH FLOODING. A FEW BARNS... HOMES AND THE LOCAL FIRE DEPARTMENT WERE FLOODED. SEVERAL COUNTY ROADS WERE WASHED OUT.
BETWEEN 500 PM AND 730 PM FLASH FLOODING OCCURRED... WITH A FEW BARNS... HOMES AND THE LOCAL FIRE DEPARTMENT FLOODED. AREA ROADS WERE WASHED AWAY.

*** 1 FATAL *** ALBUQUERQUE FIRE/RESCUE REPORTED 2 PEOPLE CAUGHT IN FLOOD WATERS IN ARROYOS. ONE PERSON WAS ABLE TO ESCAPE THE FLOOD WATERS. THE OTHER PERSONS BODY WAS RECOVERED ON JULY 28. THE LOCATION REPORTED HERE WAS WHERE THE PERSON WAS PULLED INTO THE ARROYO DURING THE STORM CAUSING HEAVY RAINFALL AND FLOODING.
